notes: First-question data regime assertion (c0) fails for BOTH with_skill and without_skill — consistent with iteration 1. Both start with defect clarification before asking about evaluation cost. The socratic_mentor_agent's Layer 1 opens with problem characterization before budget. The assertion may need to be relaxed to 'within first 2 turns' or the skill needs an explicit 'open with budget question' instruction.

thermal-monitoring with_skill improved from 4/6 to 6/6 — the physics-first organization + Model-Family Landscape structure now satisfies the ML-method-family assertion (a0) that failed in iteration 1. Key change: synthesis section now explicitly separates 6 ML families each with shared assumptions.
ml-process-control with_skill holds at 6/6 — the physics-first framing in the abstract explicitly names i.i.d. failure, sim-to-real gap physics, and engineering baseline. Strong and stable.
Numbered references [1],[2] (b4) continues to fail for without_skill runs — baseline Claude uses APA author-year. This is a clean skill discriminator.

Without_skill baseline improved on ml-process-control from 3/6 (iteration 1) to 4/6 (iteration 2) — Claude naturally characterizes data regime and names engineering baselines. The skill's marginal value is now primarily in: (1) physics-first ML-family organization, (2) assumption annotation per paper, (3) sim-to-real labeling, (4) numbered references, (5) Research Plan Summary format.

Socratic without_skill does not produce a structured Research Plan Summary (c5 FAIL) — this is a clean skill discriminator. The without_skill output ends with a table of key decisions, not a structured 5-section plan.

Token counts for with_skill runs appear anomalously low (103-417 tokens) — these are likely the agent's status notification tokens, not the full output tokens. Timing data is reliable; token counts should not be used for comparison.
